_comment: Wheel demo (circumductio, drill mode — player army only, no enemy, no auto-win): zoom on the Infantry block (seed 12345, uid 1 at ~557,300, facing south), select it, press C to wheel it right, then Z to wheel it back left. One flank file plants and holds while the rest of the block swings around it like a door on a hinge, so the line ends up facing a new bearing and lying along a new line with every man still in his rank and file — distinct from the quarter-turn (facing changes in place, block stays put) and the centre pivot (both flanks sweep about the middle). The swing is paced by the OUTER file's own jog gait (rate x radius never exceeds what the corner man can run), so this 120-man block takes ~4.5 s (~270 ticks) per 90 degree wheel; the holds are spaced to let each finish, including the ~0.2 s drill response beat before each swing starts (Unit.atomic_response_delay). Driven live through the real SelectionManager by tools/demo/DemoInputRecorder.gd; world coordinates; physics ticks (60/s).
